headline: The grueling, 630-mile road race where the only fuel is sunlight
summary: High school students will race self-built solar cars across 630 miles in Texas. The five-day challenge emphasizes student design using off-the-shelf and 3D-printed parts. Teams compete for victory powered solely by sunlight.

headline: Dave Eggers told OpenAI staff that ChatGPT was ‘silencing an entire generation’
summary: Author Dave Eggers criticized ChatGPT during a talk to OpenAI staff. He warned that AI could potentially silence an entire generation of writers. Eggers, known for supporting writers and arts, shared his concerns directly with the company.

headline: Google might not kneecap the Pixel 11a with an old processor
summary: Leaks suggest the upcoming Pixel 11a will feature a flagship-grade Tensor G6 processor. This would be a departure from the Pixel 10a, which used an older generation Tensor G4 chip. The Pixel 'a' lineup traditionally keeps modern processors, making this a welcome return.

headline: Google is open-sourcing its 3D emoji
summary: Google has made its 3D emoji available for public use in personal creations. The company shared details on the design process for the pictograms. This move was announced as part of World Emoji Day.

headline: Matt Damon Wants New Jason Bourne Movie to 'Be Like the First 3' as Work With Conclave Director Continues
summary: Matt Damon provided an update on the new Jason Bourne movie currently in development. He expressed a desire for the film to emulate the style of the original three installments. Work on the project continues with director Edward Berger.

headline: The Best Deals Today: PNY RTX 5070 OC, Apple AirPods 4, The Dark Knight Trilogy, and More
summary: A daily roundup of the best tech and entertainment deals is available today. Featured offers include the PNY RTX 5070 OC graphics card. Shoppers can also find deals on Apple AirPods 4 and The Dark Knight Trilogy.

headline: Steam Machine Engineers at Valve Signal Grim Future as Memory Crisis Is 'Still Getting Worse'
summary: Engineers at Valve, involved with Steam Machines, have issued a warning about the ongoing memory crisis. They stated the problem is 'still getting worse,' impacting tech production. Buyers face exorbitant prices due to this persistent memory shortage.

headline: Call of Duty Movie to Take Place in the Modern Warfare Universe, Director Confirms
summary: The upcoming Call of Duty movie will be set within the popular Modern Warfare universe. The film's director confirmed the universe choice for the adaptation. Paramount Pictures and Activision are collaborating on the project.

headline: Burnham will be bolder but stick to Labour manifesto, deputy leader says
summary: Incoming Prime Minister Burnham intends to pursue bolder policies in his new role. His deputy leader confirmed that these policies will adhere to the Labour manifesto. Burnham is set to replace Sir Keir Starmer after meeting King Charles III.

headline: Digital ID plan to be scrapped in Burnham's first major policy pledge
summary: Incoming Prime Minister Burnham plans to scrap the digital ID scheme. This is announced as his first significant policy pledge upon taking office. A spokesperson stated his government would focus on immediate public needs.

headline: Messi on the brink of history - will it be his last World Cup game?
summary: Argentina captain Lionel Messi is poised to play his 34th World Cup match in Sunday's final. Speculation is high regarding whether this will be his final World Cup appearance. Messi stands on the brink of making football history.

headline: Tate brothers arrested in US as further UK charges take total to 59
summary: The Tate brothers have been arrested in the United States. Further charges from the UK bring the total number of accusations against them to 59. The Crown Prosecution Service has requested their extradition from the US.
